Authors: Yundi Gong; Xiaojie Dong; Zhongyuan Xu; Guanghui Lian; Zhenghong Liu; Guoyu Fan

Journal: Journal of Asian Earth Sciences

DOI: 10.1016/j.jseaes.2026.107215

Relevance: 0.566 (consider)

The Khondalite Belt (KB) is a Paleoproterozoic collisional orogen within the North China Craton (NCC), but its pre-collisional tectonic evolution remains poorly constrained. In this study, we conducted integrated geochronological, in-situ Hf isotopic, and whole-rock geochemical analyses of the mafic–intermediate intrusions exposed in the Daqingshan area in the central segment of the KB to determine their formation ages, petrogenesis, and tectonic setting, thereby further constraining the late Paleoproterozoic tectonic evolution of the KB. Zircon U-Pb dating results show that these rocks crystallized at ∼1.97–1.96 Ga and subsequently underwent metamorphism at ∼1.94–1.92 Ga. These rocks are enriched in light rare earth elements and depleted in high field strength elements, exhibiting geochemical affinities comparable to those of continental margin arc basalts and island arc basalts. In-situ zircon Hf isotopic analyses yield positive εHf(t) values. The available data suggest that the mafic–intermediate intrusions were derived from a hybrid mantle source dominated by asthenospheric mantle and metasomatized by fluids derived from a subducted slab and experienced fractional crystallization of olivine, clinopyroxene, Fe-Ti oxides and apatite. Overall, these findings suggest that the KB was in a subduction-related tectonic setting at ∼1.97–1.96 Ga. Subsequent continent–continent collision at ∼1.95 Ga resulted in metamorphism of both the magmatic rocks and the Khondalite Series at ∼1.94–1.92 Ga.

item.title: Sub-solidus hydrology controls the formation of high-grade tin deposits in granites
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Krattiger
Jonas Köpping
Philipp Weis
Thomas Driesner
item.description: Tin deposits form primarily in and above the roof of granitic intrusions, commonly with important resources of other strategic and critical raw materials (e.g., W and Li). The metals are extracted by magmatic fluids exsolved from the crystallising intrusion; however, it is unclear how the fluids are collected and focused to form spatially confined high-grade ore bodies. Here, we present numerical simulations showing that: (1) most tin-bearing fluids that exsolve from a granitic intrusion during cooling and crystallisation are not expelled but can remain stored in the crystallising intrusion, and (2) efficient fluid mobilisation, focusing, and ore precipitation occur as naturally emerging sub-solidus processes. Hydrothermal convection in the overlying country rock – driven by the magmatic heat – cools the intrusion, which enhances permeability and induces a lateral flow of mobilised magmatic fluids towards strongly localised zones of intense magmatic-meteoric fluid mixing, an efficient mechanism for tin precipitation. Temperatures, pressures, compositions, and time scales of the simulated fluid evolution closely match field-based observations. We find that different tin precipitation mechanisms proposed in the literature ‒ fluid mixing, cooling, and boiling ‒ may all be reconciled as variations of this hydrological process in response to varying geologic conditions such as intrusion depth and/or minor variations in permeability. Our simulations show that magmatic fluids can remain stored post-crystallisation until near the ductile-to-brittle transition, thereby introducing a novel process for fluid focusing that may facilitate magmatic-hydrothermal ore formation and providing a natural interpretation for sub-solidus granite alteration. Published: 2026-07-10T10:21:51+00:00 Venue: Earth and Planetary Science Letters Score: 0.565 (consider)

Tin deposits form primarily in and above the roof of granitic intrusions, commonly with important resources of other strategic and critical raw materials (e.g., W and Li). The metals are extracted by magmatic fluids exsolved from the crystallising intrusion; however, it is unclear how the fluids are collected and focused to form spatially confined high-grade ore bodies. Here, we present numerical simulations showing that: (1) most tin-bearing fluids that exsolve from a granitic intrusion during cooling and crystallisation are not expelled but can remain stored in the crystallising intrusion, and (2) efficient fluid mobilisation, focusing, and ore precipitation occur as naturally emerging sub-solidus processes. Hydrothermal convection in the overlying country rock – driven by the magmatic heat – cools the intrusion, which enhances permeability and induces a lateral flow of mobilised magmatic fluids towards strongly localised zones of intense magmatic-meteoric fluid mixing, an efficient mechanism for tin precipitation. Temperatures, pressures, compositions, and time scales of the simulated fluid evolution closely match field-based observations. We find that different tin precipitation mechanisms proposed in the literature ‒ fluid mixing, cooling, and boiling ‒ may all be reconciled as variations of this hydrological process in response to varying geologic conditions such as intrusion depth and/or minor variations in permeability. Our simulations show that magmatic fluids can remain stored post-crystallisation until near the ductile-to-brittle transition, thereby introducing a novel process for fluid focusing that may facilitate magmatic-hydrothermal ore formation and providing a natural interpretation for sub-solidus granite alteration.

item.title: Sulphur, oxygen, and carbon isotope geochemistry of the Windy Craggy mafic-siliciclastic (Besshi-type) volcanogenic massive sulphide deposit, British Columbia
item.dc:creator: Amr Metwally
Jan M. Peter
Evelyne Leduc
Matthew I. Leybourne
item.description: The Windy Craggy deposit in northwestern British Columbia is the world’s largest mafic siliciclastic (Besshi-type) volcanogenic massive sulfide (VMS) deposit, containing ~300 Mt grading 1.38 wt.% Cu, 0.069 wt.% Co, and 0.2 g/t Au. To evaluate the sources of sulfur and carbon, and assess magmatic contributions to ore formation, we analysed sulfur, carbon, and oxygen isotopes in massive sulphide, carbonate gangue minerals, and host rocks. Whole-rock sulfur isotope compositions range from δ34S = −10.1 to +4.8‰ and are consistent with mixing between sulfur leached from footwall volcanic rocks and magmatic sulfur modified by SO2 disproportionation, with only a minor possible sedimentary contribution. Carbonate δ13C values range from −11.8 to +29.6‰ (mostly −5.9 to +7.7‰), whereas δ18O values range from +7.0 to +26.6‰. Most samples define a mixing trend between mantle-derived carbon and marine carbonate, indicating variable inputs of magmatic CO2 and seawater-derived carbon. Trace elements commonly enriched in magmatic fluids (As, Ag, Mo, Sb, and Tl) are strongly correlated and increase with decreasing δ34S. Principal component analysis similarly links these elements with δ13C and total carbon, providing compelling evidence that magmatic volatiles supplied sulfur, carbon, and metals to the Windy Craggy hydrothermal system. Published: 2026-07-11T12:07:46+00:00 Venue: International Geology Review Score: 0.563 (consider)

Authors: Amr Metwally; Jan M. Peter; Evelyne Leduc; Matthew I. Leybourne

Journal: International Geology Review

DOI: 10.1080/00206814.2026.2699156

Relevance: 0.563 (consider)

The Windy Craggy deposit in northwestern British Columbia is the world’s largest mafic siliciclastic (Besshi-type) volcanogenic massive sulfide (VMS) deposit, containing ~300 Mt grading 1.38 wt.% Cu, 0.069 wt.% Co, and 0.2 g/t Au. To evaluate the sources of sulfur and carbon, and assess magmatic contributions to ore formation, we analysed sulfur, carbon, and oxygen isotopes in massive sulphide, carbonate gangue minerals, and host rocks. Whole-rock sulfur isotope compositions range from δ34S = −10.1 to +4.8‰ and are consistent with mixing between sulfur leached from footwall volcanic rocks and magmatic sulfur modified by SO2 disproportionation, with only a minor possible sedimentary contribution. Carbonate δ13C values range from −11.8 to +29.6‰ (mostly −5.9 to +7.7‰), whereas δ18O values range from +7.0 to +26.6‰. Most samples define a mixing trend between mantle-derived carbon and marine carbonate, indicating variable inputs of magmatic CO2 and seawater-derived carbon. Trace elements commonly enriched in magmatic fluids (As, Ag, Mo, Sb, and Tl) are strongly correlated and increase with decreasing δ34S. Principal component analysis similarly links these elements with δ13C and total carbon, providing compelling evidence that magmatic volatiles supplied sulfur, carbon, and metals to the Windy Craggy hydrothermal system.

item.title: In-situ elemental compositions and U-Pb ages of garnet and vesuvianite from the Cu-Zn skarn in the Dachang Sn-polymetallic district, South China
item.dc:creator: Jiadai Li
Jing Xu
Liyuan Wang
Taiping Zhao
item.description: The Youjiang tin belt represents a globally significant Sn-polymetallic belt located at the junction between the Tethyan and Paleo-Pacific tectonic domains, yet its tectonic setting and relationship to adjacent tin belts remain poorly understood. Precise geochronological constraints are essential for deciphering ore genesis and its geodynamic evolution. This study presents U-Pb dating of garnet and vesuvianite for the Tongkeng-Yangjiaojian, Lamo and Chashan deposits within the world-class Dachang district, providing robust temporal constraints on its metallogenesis. Two garnet samples from the Tongkeng-Yangjiaojian skarns yield lower intercept ages of 92.8 ± 4.2 Ma and 92.1 ± 3.6 Ma, which are indistinguishable within analytical uncertainty from those obtained for the Lamo (93.9 ± 1.1 Ma) and Chashan (93 ± 10 Ma) deposits. In addition, vesuvianite from the Tongkeng skarn yields a U-Pb age of 90.6 ± 1.1 Ma. Trace element systematics reveal that U concentrations in all analyzed garnet samples correlate positively with LREEs rather than HREEs, indicating that U is predominantly incorporated into garnet as U4+ and occupies the X site through a mechanism similar to that governing LREEs partitioning. Positive correlations between U and W, Sn and In further suggest that a proportion of U also enters the Y site as U6+. The geochemical compositions of garnet and vesuvianite indicate that the Dachang skarn formed from the relatively oxidized, high-salinity magmatic-hydrothermal fluids. Integrated with previously published dates for the granite-related tin deposits across the adjacent Nanling Range, Southeastern Coast and Southeast Asian tin belts, these new ages support a model in which Late Cretaceous tin mineralization in the Youjiang tin belt formed in a back-arc extensional setting associated with rollback of the Neo-Tethyan oceanic slab. Published: 2026-07-16T15:35:38+00:00 Venue: Lithos Score: 0.560 (consider)

In addition, vesuvianite from the Tongkeng skarn yields a U-Pb age of 90.6 ± 1.1 Ma. item.title: Ocean acidification as a planetary signal linking Earth system memory to deep-time lithosphere–ocean geochemical interactions
item.dc:creator: Sumanta Das
Malini Roy Choudhury
item.description: Ocean acidification (OA) is widely recognized as a defining chemical signature of the Anthropocene ocean, yet it is still predominantly framed as a near-surface ecological stressor. Here, we advance a planetary-scale reinterpretation of OA as an information-bearing signal that links rapid anthropogenic carbon forcing to deep-time lithosphere–ocean geochemical interactions and long-lived Earth system memory. Observations since the late 20th century document a persistent, spatially heterogeneous decline in global surface-ocean pH, corresponding to a ∼30–35% increase in hydrogen ion concentration, driven by sustained air–sea CO2 uptake. Projected pH declines of 0.3–0.4 units by 2100 under high-emission scenarios imply rates of change at least an order of magnitude faster than most natural variations recorded in the geological archive. We synthesize carbonate system thermodynamics, carbonate compensation depth dynamics, sedimentary proxy evidence, and Earth system modeling to demonstrate how small, rapid surface perturbations propagate vertically, trigger widespread carbonate undersaturation, and imprint durable stratigraphic and geochemical signatures through sediment dissolution, altered burial fluxes, and delayed alkalinity restoration. Comparison with hyperthermal events, particularly the Paleocene–Eocene Thermal Maximum, reveals that the uniqueness of modern OA lies not in magnitude alone but in an unprecedented rate mismatch that overwhelms geological buffering mechanisms. Furthermore, we identify critical knowledge gaps regarding irreversibility, sedimentary signal emergence, and threshold behavior under rapid forcing. Overall, this study reframes OA as a planetary-scale diagnostic of whole-Earth system disequilibrium, revealing its fundamental significance for evaluating long-term carbon cycle stability, Earth system resilience, and the enduring geological legacy of anthropogenic emissions beyond human timescales. Published: 2026-07-10T23:18:16+00:00 Venue: Earth-Science Reviews Score: 0.559 (consider)

Comparison with hyperthermal events, particularly the Paleocene–Eocene Thermal Maximum, reveals that the uniqueness of modern OA lies not in magnitude alone but in an unprecedented rate mismatch that overwhelms geological buffering mechanisms. Furthermore, we identify critical knowledge gaps regarding irreversibility, sedimentary signal emergence, and threshold behavior under rapid forcing. Overall, this study reframes OA as a planetary-scale diagnostic of whole-Earth system disequilibrium, revealing its fundamental significance for evaluating long-term carbon cycle stability, Earth system resilience, and the enduring geological legacy of anthropogenic emissions beyond human timescales.

item.title: Two stages of Sn enrichment in granitic magmatism: insights from Sn isotopes of cogenetic minerals
item.dc:creator: Jia-Xin She
Weiqiang Li
Ru-Cheng Wang
Lei Xie
Chen Liu
Zhihao Xu
Shichao An
Cheng Xu
item.description: The enrichment of tin (Sn) in high-silica (high-Si) granites is commonly linked to magmatic-hydrothermal processes, yet the respective roles of mineral crystallization versus fluid activity remain debated. Direct constraints on how individual minerals partition Sn and fractionate Sn isotopes during granite differentiation are particularly scarce. Here we report Sn concentrations and stable Sn isotope compositions (δ 122/118Sn3161a) for cogenetic minerals (muscovite, biotite, tourmaline, feldspar, quartz, spodumene, and cassiterite) and whole rocks from the compositionally zoned Pusila leucogranite pluton in the Himalaya. Tin is heterogeneously distributed among minerals: muscovite acts as the principal host and is isotopically heavier than the host rocks, whereas feldspar and quartz contain only minor Sn and are generally isotopically lighter. Cassiterite from pegmatites is also isotopically light, with a mean δ 122/118Sn3161a of 0.036‰. Whole‑rock δ 122/118Sn3161a values increase systematically from −0.035‰ in biotite granite to + 0.337‰ in albite granite, whereas pegmatites show larger Sn isotope variations (0.292‰ to 0.816‰). These observations reveal two distinct stages of Sn behavior. During the magmatic stage, progressive crystallization of feldspar preferentially removes light Sn isotopes from the melt, driving the residual magma toward heavier isotopic compositions while allowing Sn to behave incompatibly. In the pegmatite stage, low Nb/Ta, Zr/Hf, and K/Rb ratios, pronounced REE tetrad effects, and widespread sericitization indicate significant fluid activity. An exsolved Sn-bearing aqueous fluid precipitates isotopically light cassiterite in the pegmatites, leaving the residual melt isotopically heavier. Together, these results support a model in which Sn enrichment involves early magmatic pre‑concentration followed by fluid‑mediated redistribution and cassiterite precipitation. Our findings delineate the mechanisms of Sn enrichment in evolved granitic systems and demonstrate the potential of Sn isotopes as a tracer of magmatic-hydrothermal processes. Published: 2026-07-14T23:19:43+00:00 Venue: Geochimica et Cosmochimica Acta Score: 0.559 (consider)

The enrichment of tin (Sn) in high-silica (high-Si) granites is commonly linked to magmatic-hydrothermal processes, yet the respective roles of mineral crystallization versus fluid activity remain debated. In the pegmatite stage, low Nb/Ta, Zr/Hf, and K/Rb ratios, pronounced REE tetrad effects, and widespread sericitization indicate significant fluid activity. Together, these results support a model in which Sn enrichment involves early magmatic pre‑concentration followed by fluid‑mediated redistribution and cassiterite precipitation. item.title: Early diagenetic dolomitization in methane seep carbonates constrained by coupled Mo and Mg isotopes
item.dc:creator: Meng Jin
Zice Jia
Jie Li
Xun Sun
Shaomin Lin
Jörn Peckmann
Qinxian Wang
Duofu Chen
Dong Feng
item.description: Marine carbonates are widely regarded as excellent archives for recording the geochemical evolution of seawater. However, the long-standing “dolomite problem” has hindered the reconstruction of formation mechanisms and depositional settings of the widespread occurrences of dolomite throughout Earth’s history. Cold seeps, where sulfate-driven anaerobic oxidation of methane (SD-AOM) promotes the formation of dolomite, are key to deciphering low-temperature dolomitization processes. Here, we investigate late Miocene to early Pleistocene seep dolomite from Chiahsien, Taiwan, together with modern seep dolomite from the Gulf of Mexico and South China Sea, using a coupled molybdenum (Mo) and magnesium (Mg) isotope approach to constrain the dolomitization environment and processes within seep systems. The wide range of authigenic Mo isotope compositions (δ98Moauth: 0.29 to 2.94‰), correlating with Fe/Al ratios, tracks changes of methane flux spanning from diffusion-dominated (enrichment of heavy Mo) to oxide-shuttle-dominated (enrichment of light Mo) regimes. This contrasts with the homogeneous δ26Mg values of seep dolomite (−2.72 ± 0.21‰), reflecting equilibration with seawater during dolomitization in a shallow, porewater environment with high replenishment of seawater Mg. The Chiahsien seep dolomite further displays higher crystallographic ordering expressed as I(015)/I(110), generally lower Sr/(Mg + Ca) ratios, and more euhedral crystals than modern seep dolomite. Taken together, our new observations suggest that precursor carbonate minerals (aragonite, calcite) were dolomitized during early diagenesis at shallow depth. The lack of Rayleigh fractionation effects of Mg isotope distinguishes seep dolomite from dolomite formed in more restricted, deeper settings, such as environments dominated by organic sulfate reduction and methanogenesis. This study demonstrates that combined Mo–Mg isotope systematics can effectively decipher the redox history and the process of dolomitization. Furthermore, it suggests that seep dolomite, which tends to form in open, seawater-buffered porewater environments, is a promising archive for reconstructing the Mg isotope composition of ancient seawater. Published: 2026-07-13T05:46:57+00:00 Venue: Geochimica et Cosmochimica Acta Score: 0.556 (consider)

Marine carbonates are widely regarded as excellent archives for recording the geochemical evolution of seawater. This contrasts with the homogeneous δ26Mg values of seep dolomite (−2.72 ± 0.21‰), reflecting equilibration with seawater during dolomitization in a shallow, porewater environment with high replenishment of seawater Mg. The Chiahsien seep dolomite further displays higher crystallographic ordering expressed as I(015)/I(110), generally lower Sr/(Mg + Ca) ratios, and more euhedral crystals than modern seep dolomite. Taken together, our new observations suggest that precursor carbonate minerals (aragonite, calcite) were dolomitized during early diagenesis at shallow depth. The lack of Rayleigh fractionation effects of Mg isotope distinguishes seep dolomite from dolomite formed in more restricted, deeper settings, such as environments dominated by organic sulfate reduction and methanogenesis. This study demonstrates that combined Mo–Mg isotope systematics can effectively decipher the redox history and the process of dolomitization. item.title: Complexly zoned clinopyroxenes and amphiboles from the end-Cretaceous alkaline lamprophyres of the Nir-Wandh igneous complex, NW Deccan Traps, India: Insights into trans-lithospheric magmatic plumbing system processes
item.dc:creator: Abhinay Sharma
Rohit Pandey
N.V. Chalapathi Rao
A.D. Shukla
Samarendra Sahoo
Prashant Dhote
item.description: The Nir-Wandh Igneous Complex (67 Ma) of the Kutch region, NW India, represents one of the earliest manifestations of alkaline magmatism within the Deccan Large Igneous Province. This study investigates the petrology and geochemistry of four camptonitic alkaline lamprophyre dykes which are characterized by complexly zoned clinopyroxenes and amphiboles, with minor apatite, FeTi oxides, and feldspar confined to the groundmass. Based on the presence or absence of olivine and the nature of clinopyroxene populations, the four lamprophyre dykes are divided into three groups: Group-A, Group-B, and Group-C. Four clinopyroxene varieties are identified: (i) P1-zoned colourless core phenocrysts with Cr-Mg-rich diopside cores and Ti-rich augite rims; (ii) P2-unzoned colourless Fe-diopside phenocrysts; (iii) P3-green core antecrysts zoned from Na-rich salite to diopside; and (iv) P4-grey core antecrysts zoned from salite to diopside. P1 occurs in Groups A and B, both P3 and P4 are restricted to the Group A, and Group C contains only P2. Three amphibole parageneses are also recognised, ranging from Ti-rich ferro-pargasite and kaersutite to Ti-rich magnesio-hastingsite, each with distinct host-rock associations. Bulk-rock geochemistry and isotopic compositions 87Sr/86Sri (0.70407 to 0.70441) and 143Nd/144Ndi (0.51279 to 0.51286) are similar across the lamprophyres and associated rocks, indicating a common origin. Trace-element modelling of melts in equilibrium with clinopyroxene suggests involvement of two distinct pulses of co-genetic magmas: (i) a camptonitic magma and (ii) a Na-Nb-Zr enriched evolved magma. Amphibole zoning and disequilibrium textures record inherited Fe-rich antecrystic cores and later Mg-rich phenocrystic rims, reflecting interaction between evolved and replenishing magmas. Textural evidence, mineral chemistry, and P-T estimates indicate prolonged fractionation, magma recharge and mixing, crystal recycling, and accumulation within multiple trans-lithospheric reservoirs. Thermobarometry suggests that the Kutch lithosphere was at least ~70 km thick during emplacement. These results demonstrate that pyroxene and amphibole zoning in lamprophyres provides valuable constraints on magma generation depths, mixing processes, and the architecture of trans-lithospheric magmatic plumbing systems. Published: 2026-07-16T05:20:42+00:00 Venue: Lithos Score: 0.552 (consider)
